versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 28 Concerning Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor, which Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon defeated, this is what the LORD says:
and destroy the people of the east!
29 They will take their tents and flocks,
their tent curtains and all their goods.
They will take their camels for themselves.
They will shout to them: ‘Terror is on every side!’
Lie low, O residents of Hazor,”
has drawn up a plan against you;
he has devised a strategy against you.
31 Rise up, advance against a nation at ease,
and their large herds will be spoil.
I will scatter to the wind in every direction
I will bring calamity on them
33 “Hazor will become a haunt for jackals,

context_lines: In **Jeremiah 49**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Jeremiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **Judgment on Kedar and Hazor** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Jeremiah 49** remains the first control on meaning.
